question: Is the manual toothbrush in the cup successfully replaced with the white electric toothbrush (featuring a blue accent ring) from the reference image?
Does the mirror reflection accurately display the electric toothbrush instead of the manual toothbrush?
Does the orientation, angle, and scale of the electric toothbrush in the mirror reflection match the geometry of the foreground object?
Does the electric toothbrush appear naturally inserted into the cup, with the cup rim properly occluding the lower part of the handle?
Is the electric toothbrush positioned with natural spacing, avoiding unnatural overlap or crowding with the adjacent toothpaste tube?
Is the lighting on the electric toothbrush adjusted to match the warm, yellowish ambient light of the bathroom scene (avoiding a cold studio look)?
Are there realistic contact shadows visible where the electric toothbrush meets the rim or interior of the cup?
Are small water droplets visible on the body of the electric toothbrush?
Do the water droplets appear to conform to the curved surface of the toothbrush handle and reflect the scene lighting?
Are the water droplets subtle and consistent with a moist atmosphere, rather than exaggerated or excessive in size/quantity?
Is the toothpaste tube next to the toothbrush preserved without distortion or unintended modification?
Are the sink, faucet, and bathroom wall reflection preserved without unintended changes?
Is the boundary of the inserted toothbrush free from harsh seams, halos, or pixelated edges?
Is the electric toothbrush free from structural distortions (e.g., warped handle or deformed brush head)?
